gene_description: Melanocortin-4 Receptor — critical regulator of appetite, energy homeostasis, and body weight in the hypothalamus. Variants near MC4R are consistently associated with increased BMI and obesity risk across populations.

source.title: MC4R rs489693: a clinical risk factor for second generation antipsychotic-related weight gain?
source.finding: MC4R rs489693 A-allele carriers show 2.2-3.1 times higher weight gain with antipsychotic use. The variant also associates with higher BMI, triglycerides, leptin, and insulin levels in the general population.

AA.effect: Homozygous risk allele carriers show 2.2-3.1 fold higher weight gain, particularly with antipsychotic medications. Elevated triglycerides, leptin, and insulin levels. Increased appetite drive and reduced satiety signaling through melanocortin pathway disruption.
AA.recommendation: Focus on caloric deficit strategies and high-protein diets to enhance satiety. Monitor metabolic parameters closely if on antipsychotics. GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide, tirzepatide) may be particularly beneficial. Regular physical activity strongly recommended.

AC.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate weight gain susceptibility. Mildly elevated appetite drive. Modest increase in antipsychotic-related weight gain risk.
AC.recommendation: Standard dietary and lifestyle recommendations with emphasis on satiety-promoting foods. Monitor weight if starting antipsychotic medications.

CA.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate weight gain susceptibility. Mildly elevated appetite drive. Modest increase in antipsychotic-related weight gain risk.
CA.recommendation: Standard dietary and lifestyle recommendations with emphasis on satiety-promoting foods. Monitor weight if starting antipsychotic medications.

CC.effect: Wild-type with standard melanocortin signaling. Normal appetite regulation and energy homeostasis from this locus.
CC.recommendation: Standard dietary guidelines. Normal expected weight response to medications.

AA.effect: Homozygous minor allele carriers show increased BMI and higher overweight/obesity risk. Associated with higher energy intake in some populations.
AA.recommendation: Calorie-controlled diet recommended. Emphasis on portion control and regular meal timing. Monitor weight trends.

GA.effect: Heterozygous carriers show marginally increased BMI tendency.
GA.recommendation: Standard dietary and lifestyle recommendations. Maintain regular physical activity.

AG.effect: Heterozygous carriers show marginally increased BMI tendency.
AG.recommendation: Standard dietary and lifestyle recommendations. Maintain regular physical activity.

GG.effect: Wild-type at this locus. Standard BMI and appetite regulation.
